name: bioethics_newsroom_s_automated_millhaven_auto_ben
premise: A Bioethics ethics consult line is in a fast-moving incident involving bioethics newsroom s automated millhaven auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases consent, harm minimization, and precedent. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Darius Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Darius Kowalski, accountable for a defensible call in Bioethics when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Ansel Mensah
agents.identity: I am Ansel Mensah, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Bioethics.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Darius Kowalski must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Darius Kowalski do?
